description: Jira analysis quality benchmark — autoresearch-style evaluation

benchmark_issues.notes: Epic with 21 child issues covering medlemskap/trygdeavgift
User story with acceptance criteria, references Confluence docs
Large issue with test failures, rework cycles, stakeholder feedback
619 lines, reproduction steps, Postman scripts, multi-stakeholder analysis
EU/EOS utsending epic, QA feedback workflow
EFTA/Storbritannia workflow, regulatory nuances (EOS vs Konvensjon)
Minimal description, Figma references only — tests sparse-issue handling
Small focused CI fix, technical backlog
Spans journalforing, EESSI, SED — multi-system complexity, long-lived issue
Orphan bug, no epic, sparse description — baseline complexity
Large sub-task from early development, technical complexity
Research/analysis issue — domain knowledge over code
Production bug, SED/EESSI domain, long resolution time (2020-2024)

configurations.name: with-wiki
configurations.description: Add curated wiki for conceptual understanding

dimensions.name: domain_understanding
dimensions.description: Do the search results explain the domain concepts referenced in the issue (lovvalg, trygdeavtaler, BUC/SED types, forordninger, etc.)?

dimensions.name: technical_context
dimensions.description: Are relevant technical docs, architecture decisions, and implementation guides found?

dimensions.name: related_work
dimensions.description: Are related Jira issues, epics, and cross-references found?

dimensions.name: actionability
dimensions.description: Could an agent create a concrete work plan from these search results? Do they point to specific files, services, or APIs?

dimensions.name: noise_ratio
dimensions.description: What proportion of results are actually relevant? Low noise = high score.
